O mother-in-law! you are ever thinking of the stomach! 3. Āp na hāreṅ dhandā kartī, Purkhan tāīṅ harā āveṅ, Chal sāthan! bīj ghalā āveṅ! Rus. Wom. song. I never tire my work to do, The elders I tire out all through. Come, dear! let's up the seed to sow! 4. Dhande meṅ dhan basat hai jūṅ paṅkhe meṅ paun, Hile jule se det hai, na to deve kaun? Dohā. Wealth dwells in work as wind comes from a fan, Who plieth gets, without work gives no man.

Maiṅ kātūṅ, tauṅ pīs rī, sāsar, donon mil ghar kār kareṅ, Pīyā, susar, jab ye ḍhaṅg dekheṅ, bahuta ham nūṅ piyār kareṅ . W. Rus. song. I'll spin, you grind, my mother-in-law! together work we two, Our husbands seeing us at work will greatly love us two. (She assigns the harder work to her mother-in-law). 5. Property; phenomenon. 6. Art; accomplishment. Kyā mukh le ghar jāūṅ pīyā ke, Naīhar meṅ kachhu ḍhaṅg na sīkho ! Bhajan. Before my Lord how can I show my face? A body, band, party, side, faction; — a weight (usually of ten ser), or the quantity weighed by it; a counter-balancing weight; a large weight made up by counter-balancing stones, &c. against a small weight, and adding them to that weight, and so on, till the original weight is doubled, or quadrupled, or any required standard of weight is obtained
more: s. m. 1. A party. 2. A weight. دهڙا باندهنا dhaṛā bāndhnā, To make up a standard to weigh by, with bricks or any thing else; as, by having one weight (of a pound, suppose) it is doubled, quadrupled, and so on, till the weight requisite is obtained.
subs: (-kā), To weigh (by means of a dhaṛā)
v.n. To make up a party or side; — to make up a standard to weigh with (see dhaṛā)
or paṛnā, v.n. To be split up into bands, or parties, or factions
To frame or bring a counter charge.

r: dhāṛā, dhāṛ, dhāṛnā, dhāṛ paṛnā, par, dhāṛ denā, dhāṛ mārnā
def: s.m. = H دهاڙ धाड़ dhāṛ, s.f. (fr. dhāṛnā, q.v.), A multitude, crowd, band, gang; a band or gang of robbers, banditti; — a robbery; — a stream or body of water falling from a height, a torrent, a water-fall
more: s. m. A crowd, a multitude.
subs: (-par), A band of robbers to fall (upon), to be ataacked by a band of robbers
dhāṛ mārnā, To plunder in bands.

def: s.f. The earth
more: n. m. 1. (S. धट a balance.) Balancing a weight. 2. ( dhaṛ ) A party; side; faction. dhaṛā uṭhānā , v. n. To weigh ( tolnā , 1). dhaṛā aur kardā , n. m. Tare and tret. dhaṛā bāṅdhnā , v. n. To make up a side or party. dhaṛā k. , v. n. To balance; equipoise. Pahle dhaṛā kar lo. dhaṛe baṅdhnā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To be broken up or divided into factions or parties. Birādrī meṅ dhaṛe paṛ rahe haiṅ. ulṭā dhaṛā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To bring a countercharge. ṭake dhaṛā lagānā , v. n. lit . to sell at five seers the ṭakā . To sell off, or sell cheap.
subs: s.m. Earth, ground; surface, superficies, table
s.m. 'God of the earth,' an epithet of a Brāhman.

def: adv. & s.f. = H دهڙادهڙي धड़ाधड़ी dhaṛā-dhaṛī, s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.
s.f. = H دهڙادهڙ धड़ाधड़ dharā-dhaṛ, adv. & s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ.

ety: dharā, i.q. dharāʼo, q.v.+har = Prk. घरं = S. गृहं
def: s.m. A depository.
s.f. Placing, putting down; holding, laying hold of, seizing.
adj. & s.m. Holding, containing; — what or who holds, a receptacle; a debtor.
s.f. Beating, throbbing, palpitating; beat, throb, palpitation; trepidation, fear, alarm (cf. thalak).
s.m. A loud report, explosion, crash, peal, raging (of a tempest), violence (of an attack, &c.), din and roar (of a battle); vehemence; rapidity; boldness
more: s. m. A crash, report of a gun, explosion.
subs: adv. With a loud report, with a crash; boldly, fearlessly; rapidly, quickly.

def: n. m. dhaṛā dhaṛ, dhaṛā-dhaṛī , n. f. A crash; report of a gun; explosion. 1. Jhaṛyoṅ ne is tarah kā dīyā ā-ke jhaṛ lagā, Sunye jidhar udhar ko dhaṛāke kī hai sadā . Nazīr. 2. Barso Rām dhaṛāke se, tūmbā bhar de āṭe se! Mendicants. O Rām! send rain in torrents down, my calabash with wheat meal fill! dhaṛāke se , adv. 1. With a crash. 2. Quickly. 3. Boldly. Dhaṛāke se yeh kām bhī kar do!
s.m. the Urdū form of the next, q.v.
s.m. A scarecrow ( = ḍhaṛkhā, q.v.). s.m. Trepidation, alarm ( = dhaṛkā); — a scarecrow ( = dhaṛak-kā, q.v.).
s.m. Report, explosion, crash ( = dhaṛākā, q.v.); sound of hammering, &c.; alarming, frightening, intimidating, bullying; bravado (cf. dharsh); — a crowd ( = dhaṛallā; bhīṛ).
v.n. = dhaṛaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. To palpitate; throb; beat.

ety: dhaṛak˚ = Prk. दड्ढक्क(इ), fr. S. दग्ध(p.p. of rt. दह्)+कृ
def: v.n. To blaze up, to burn fiercely; to be intensely hot (as the body in fever, &c.); to be inflamed (with anger, or lust, &c.; cf. bhaṛaknā); — to be distressed, to tremble (from fear, &c.); to beat, throb, palpitate, flutter.
more: v. n. To palpitate. To blaze(?).

def: v.t. = ḍhalaknā, q.v. v.n. To run down, to flow; to be poured out, be spilt, be shed; to fall or hang down loosely, be flaccid or loose (cf. laṭaknā); to decline, sink, fade ( = ḍhalnā); to rock; to roll, slide ( = ḍhulaknā)
s. (dakh.) A crashing noise ( Harris ).
s.m. Sound of beating or hammering, &c. ( = dhaṛakkā, q.v.); — a crowd, band, host, swarm ( = bhīṛ); — a scarecrow.
more: s. m. 1. The sound of hammering, &c. 2. Frightening, alarming, bullying, bravado. 3. A crowd.

def: n. m. H. dhāṛ . a crowd. A band; swarm ( bhīṛ ). Dhaṛalle ke dhaṛalle chale āte haiṅ .
n. The sound of a heavy body falling into water, or on soft ground. Pānī meṅ dhaṛām se kūd paṛā .
adv. With a crash, &c.

s.f. The earth ( = dharanī); the womb; the navel, the umbilical cord or vein (supposed by Hindū physicians to become occasionally displaced, and thus to give rise to various morbid symptoms); — a beam; — accent, tone
subs: dharan ḍignā or haṭnā, The womb, or the navel, to be displaced.

def: s.m. Holding, bearing, carrying, keeping, wearing; upholding, maintaining, sustaining, preserving; possessing, having, assuming; knowing, acquiring; undertaking
more: ( In comp ). S. धा to hold, bear. Holding; bearing. dhāran k., dhārnā , v. n. 1. To assume; adopt; take on oneself; bear. Phr. To commit oneself to a course. 2. To put on; wear. Janeo dhāran kīyā. dhār'nā , v. n. To pour (water). dūsrā mat dhāran k , v. n. To embrace a different faith.
verbal n. s. Holding, bearing, sustaining, upholding, assuming, keeping. See دهارنا dhārnā.
subs: v.t. To bear; to possess; to wear; to assume; to seize, retain; to draw, to absorb, &c.

def: s.m. Holding, seizing; having, possessing, keeping; bearing, supporting; placing; fastening, &c. (see dharnā); — a support; a fastening; a bank, dike, dam; — the female breast; — a sort of measure or weight.
more: n. f. S. धरण 1. The earth. 2. The womb. 3. A beam. 4. Accent; tone. dharan ḍignā, haṭnā, yā ṭalnā , v. n. To be moved (the womb or the navel from its place). dharan laṛānā yā milānā , Slang. v. a. To come in contact (in sexual intercourse).

s. f. 1. ( धरण ) A beam. 2. Accent, tone. 3. The navel (or rather the umbilical vein, perhaps the aorta or cœliack artery, as it is said to pulsate) which is supposed by the Hindū physicians to be occasionally removed from its place, and thus to occasion various morbid symptoms. This is called دهرن ڐگني dharan-ḍignī or ناف ٿلنا يا اکهڙنا nāf-ṭalnā or -ukhaṛnā, and the cure is attempted by friction of the belly. 4. See دهرنا dharnā.
s. m. Placing, putting, &c.: see دهرنا dharnā.

def: s.f. Keeping in remembrance, retaining in the mind; a good memory, memory; — continuance in rectitude, keeping in the right way, fortitude, firmness, steadiness, resolution; aptitude to acquire (wisdom, &c.).
v.t. To owe ( = dhārnā); — to deposit, to give in deposit ( = dharnā); — v.n. (prov.) To be laid hold of, be seized, be appre hended, be imprisoned.
more: v. a. To owe. Maiṅ kuchh tumhārā dharātā hūṅ!

def: s.m. Body and limbs; — (dialec.) adj. Large, lusty, stalwart, strapping
subs: 'naked in body and limb,' stark naked).

def: s. (dakh.) Placer, placing.
s.f. The earth, globe; earth, soil, land, ground; — a beam
subs: s.m. lit. 'Bearing or sustaining the earth'; an epithet of Śesh-nāg, the serpent which is said to uphold the earth; — an epithet of Vishnu in the form of a tortoise and a boar; — a mountain
s.f. 'Daughter of the earth,' an epithet of Sītā wife of Rāma (as produced from the soil by Janaka when he was ploughing a spot to prepare it for sacrifice)
s.m. An esculent root or bulb.

def: s.f. A straight line; any tubular vessel of the body.
s.m. = dharāʼī, q.v. s.f. Placing, putting down; holding, laying hold of, seizing.
s.f. (m. ?) = ḍhāras, q.v.
s.f.m.(?), Firmness of mind, confidence, courage, boldness, heart, encouragement, comfort; fortitude, patience (cf. dhīraj)
more: s. f. m. Firmness of mind, confidence, animation, encouragement, comfort. ڐهاڙس بندهانا ḍhāṛas bandhānā, v. a. To encourage, to comfort, to keep in spirits. ڐهاڙس دينا ḍhāṛas denā, v. a. To animate.
subs: To keep up the spirits, to take heart, have courage, or patience, &c.
(-kī), or ḍhāṛas denā (-ko), To impart courage (to), encourage, animate, cheer, comfort.

def: n. f. S. दार्ढ्य fixedness. See dhīraj . Are bachche! tanne kime bī ḍhāras nāë! Rus. Child! you have n't the least patience. ḍhāras bāṅdh'nā , v. n. To take courage; to brace the nerves. ḍhāras denā, yā bāṅdhnā . See dhīraj baṅdhānā .
s. (dakh.) See ڐهاڙس ḍhāṛas.
= ḍhāṛas, q.v.
v.t. To overpower; to repress, restrain, curb, chide.
s.f. Ascertainment by guess or conjecture.
s.m. = dharāʼonā, q.v.
more: n. m. H. droh fraud. A second marriage of a girl brought about by her father. If the father of a girl has betrothed his daughter to a person and received the ṭīkā or wedding or betrothal gifts, and afterwards treacherously betroths and marries the girl to another person, the father is said to have committed dharonā .

def: s.f. Land ascertained and apportioned by estimate (not by measurement).
s.f. A woman married a second time; second marriage of a girl.

ety: dhar, prob. rt., or past. conj. part., of dharnā, or = dhaṛ, q.v.; and dhamaknā, q.v.
def: v.n. To come or move on rapidly, to proceed with tumultuous rapidity, to rush off or forward, to set off in hot haste, to start off (e.g. dhar-dhamkā wahāṅ se laṛtā-huʼā shahr-kī t̤araf. — Saudā).
more: v. n. To run swiftly; go with a rush.
v. n. To proceed with tumultuous rapidity, to move with violence, to rush.

def: adv. & s.f. See s.v. dhaṛ. s.f. A continuous line, a line, lineament; a line or chain (of mountains, &c.); edge (of a sword, &c.); sharpness; — flowing, flow, stream, current; a line or stream of descending fluid (as milk from a cow, &c.; hence), a stream of milk, milk; a libation
subs: s.f. An even or general distribution of charges, rates, &c.
(or pe) mārnā, v.t. To disregard, contemn, despise, scorn (cf. dhār-mārnā)
adj. & s.m. Edged, sharp; — an edged or sharp tool or weapon
or dhār-dhūrā, s.m. Boundary (between two villages); boundary formed by a stream
(-par, or -kī), To give an edge (to), to sharpen; — to winnow
To give a stream of milk, to give milk
v.n. The edge to be turned or to become jagged
(-kī), To receive, or to drink, milk as it streams from the teat
(-par), To pass urine, to make water (on); to despise, scorn, &c. (see dhār-par māṛnā)
(-kī), To milk; — to produce an edge (on), to sharpen.

def: v.n. To knock (at a door); to sound (a drum), make a rattling or banging noise; — to flutter, to beat, to palpitate.
more: v. n. To flutter, to palpitate.

ety: dharā, p. p. of dharnā+Prk. इल्ली or इली = S. इली
def: s.f. (dialec.) A kept woman, a mistress, a concubine.
s. (dakh. i. q. خاطرجمعي) Comfort, confidence, ease of mind.
s.f. = ḍhāṛas, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. धृष्ट, rt. धृ
def: s.m. A caste of (Mohammadan) singers (among the retainers of kings and great men, whom they precede on their march with armies, or in processions, chanting praises, &c.); an individual of that caste; a singer, a musician.
more: s. m. A kind of musician, a singer.

def: s.f. = ḍhāṛin, q.v. s.f. A woman of the ḍhāṛī caste; the wife of a ḍhāṛī.
more: s. f. A kind of musician, a singer.

def: adj. & s.m. Bearing, holding, carrying, wearing, having, keeping, possessing; sustaining, supporting, maintaining, preserving, &c.; — bearer, holder, &c. (used as last member of comp., e.g. jīv-dhārī).
s.f. The plant Lythrum fructicosum (used in dyeing); the plant Grislea tomentosa ( = dhātakī).
s.f. A line; border or edge (of cloth, &c.)
subs: (with loc.), To mark lines (on the teeth) with missī, to apply missī (to the teeth or lips).

def: s.m. = ḍhāṛī, q.v. s.f. A line; lineament; a stripe, a streak; a groove; — a small buttress; an army, a host; — margin, extremity, border
n. m. ḍhāṛin , n. f. A sect of musicians or singers. Ranḍī kī kamāī yā khāë ḍhāṛī, yā khāë gāṛī . Prov. The prostitute's earnings go to the musician or the cabman.
s.f. A weight of five ser or seers; the quantity weighed at a time, five seers; — a large weight made up, &c. (see dhaṛā); — five hundred rupees
more: s. f. 1. A line, particularly the black lines made on the lips with missī. 2. A weight of five ser. 3. The quantity weighed at once.
subs: To spoil (one) of everything, to make a clean sweep (of).

ety: prob. fr. S. धृष्ट, rt. धृ
def: s.m. A caste of (Mohammadan) singers (among the retainers of kings and great men, whom they precede on their march with armies, or in processions, chanting praises, &c.); an individual of that caste; a singer, a musician.
more: s. m. A kind of musician, a singer.

def: s.f. A line; lineament; a stripe, a streak; a groove; — a small buttress; an army, a host; — margin, extremity, border
more: n. f. धार A line ( khat ); streak; stripe. dhārī-dār , adj. Lined; striped; streaked.

s. f. 1. A line, lineament. 2. A small buttress. دهاري دار dhārī-dār, adj. Lined, striped, streaked. 3. (Sans. धातकी ) Name of a plant used in dying. (Lythrum fruticosum: Linn. Grislea tomentosa: Roxb. Pl. Cor. ). 4. (from धृ Hold, bear) used in compos. Bearing, holding, having, wearing.

def: s.m. The second husband of a Hindū widow among the lower classes (this connexion being contrary to the spirit of the Hindū institutions, is formed without any particular ceremony, except marking the bride's head with minium).
more: n. m. H. dharnā to keep. The second husband of a Hin. widow among the lower classes.

s. m. The second husband of a Hindū widow among the lower classes. (This connexion, being contrary to the spirit of the Hindū institutions, is formed without any particular ceremony, except marking the brideʼs head with minium).

def: s.f. A woman of the ḍhāṛī caste; the wife of a ḍhāṛī.
more: s. f. A female of a ڐهاڙي q. v.

def: s.m. (local), A kind of rice.
s.m. Name of a subordinate Hindū caste.
part. Borne, supported, upheld, maintained, sustained; assumed; — s.m. A horse's foot.
s.f. A female bearer, supporter; the earth.
s.m. Beating, throb, &c. ( = dhaṛak); suspense, doubt, fear, apprehension; — crash, peal; thunder; shock, blow (cf. dhakkā, dhaćkā).
more: s. m. 1. Fear, doubt, suspense. 2. Palpitation. 3. Thunder.

def: n. m. 1. Alarm (انديشه 1.) 2. Palpitation.
s.f. = dhaṛak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Holding, containing; — what or who holds, a receptacle; a debtor.
wety: Related to دَھڑَکنا (dhaṛaknā, “to beat, throb”), from Prakrit *𑀥𑀟𑁆 (*dhaḍ, “beat”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-).

def: n. f. Palpitation; throbbing.
v.t. = ḍhalkānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to run over or down, to cause to flow; to spill; to pour out; to tilt, overturn; to cause to roll, to roll ( = ḍhulkānā)
v. a. To alarm. Caus. of dhaṛaknā , q. v. Tumhārī bāteṅ aur jī dhaṛkātī haiṅ .
v.t. To cause to beat or palpitate, to put in a state of trepidation, to frighten, alarm.

ety: Prk. डड्ढकाअओ = S. दग्धकाक+कः; cf. daheṛ
def: s.m. A raven.
s.m. Trepidation, alarm ( = dhaṛkā); — a scarecrow ( = dhaṛak-kā, q.v.).
s.m. A scarecrow ( = dhokhā).
n. m. S. दर + कथ A scarecrow.
s.m. A scarecrow ( = ḍharkhā).

r: dharma, dharm, dharam, lit, dharmāt, dharmātmā, ˚ma, āt˚
def: s.m.f. Religious, pious, virtuous, just; — a pious or virtuous person, a saint; — the Holy Spirit; — s.f. Justice
more: n. m. S. धृ to hold, or bear. 1. See ايمان and پن punn . 1. Dharam kā saudā hai! Conscience wares sold here! 2. Rupaë meṅ do āne to dharam ke haiṅ . Two annas in the rupee conscience may fairly claim. 3. Atishaë dekhī dharm kī hānī, Param sabhīt dharā akulānī . Rāmāyan. Religion from the earth quite fled, Earth shrinking saw and shook with dread. 4. Jo tumhāre dharm meṅ āë so de do . 2. Religious observances; caste ceremonies. 3. Duty; obligation. 4. Caste. Jel-khāne meṅ kisī dharm nahīṅ rahtā! dharm-updesh , n. m. Moral or religious instruction. dharm-updeshak , n. m. A gurū or spiritual preceptor. dharm-ātmā , adj. See punyātmā under پن dharmā-dharmī , n. f. Swearing to another. dharm-adhikārī, dharm-ādhīksh , n. m. A superior over Brahmans, invested with power to investigate and chastise breaches of custom, violations of caste, etc. dharm-ārth , n. m. A charitable grant; religious end…

s. m. 1. Justice, virtue, religion. 2. Usage, practice, the customary observances of cast, sect, &c. 3. Duty, especially that enjoined by the Vedas. دهرم باپ dharm-bāp, An adopted father, a godfather. دهرم بيٿا dharm-beṭā, Adopted son. دهرم دهاري धर्मधारी dharm-dhārī, A maintainer of justice. دهرم راج dharm-rāj, s. m. 1. धर्मराज Justking; Yama or Pluto. 2. (for धर्मराज्य ) A kingdom where justice is administered. دهرم راج کرنا dharm-rāj karnā, To govern justly. دهرم سالا ( धर्मशाला ) dharam- or dharm-sālā, s. m. A place where alms are distributed.
subs: s.m. The observance of religion or of prescribed duty, &c. (whether ceremonial or otherwise); religiousness of behaviour; — dharm-āćār or dharm-āćārī, s.m. (f. -ini), One who practises the principles or the ceremonies of religion, &c. (see dharm-ćārī)
(˚ma+adh˚), s.m. Religion and irreligion; right and wrong; justice and injustice
s.f. lit. 'Test of right and wrong'; a kind of ordeal by drawing lots or slips of black and white paper
vulg. dharm-adhikār (˚ma+adh˚), s.m. The administration of justice; the right to administer law; the office of a judge
vulg. dharm-adhikārī, and dharmādhyaksh (˚ma+adh˚), vulg. dharmādhīksh, s.m. Minister of justice, chief officer of justice, an administrator of the law, a judge, magistrate
(˚ma+ar˚), adv. For the sake of justice or religion, for religious purposes; — s.m. A charitable grant, religious endowment
(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. One who is jealous for justice or religion
(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. A sacred grove or wood; name of a sacred forest in Madhyadeś into which Dharma is said to have retired
(˚ma+ās˚), s.m. Throne of justice, judgment seat, the bench
adj. & s.m. Having a passion for religion, zealous, enthusiastic in religious matters; — a religious zealot or enthusiast
(˚ma+an˚), s.m. Conformity to law or virtue; course or practice of duty; — adv. In conformity to law, &c., according to law or duty &c.
vulg. dharm-autār (˚ma+av˚), s.m. Incarnation of Dharma, or god of justice (an epithet of respect for a just or upright man); your holiness; your excellency; his holiness, &c.
v.n. To swear by (one's) creed, to take an oath
adj. Contrary to law or justice, opposed to religion or duty, unlawful or unjust; impious, immoral
s.f. A virtuous and amiable wife
s.m. A just or virtuous man
adj. Cherishing the observance of the law, observing the law, dutiful
s.m. A deed of gift or endowment for a religious purpose; — the glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata
s.m. A lawful son, a son begot from a sense of duty; 'son of the god Dharma,' an epithet of the Pānḍu prince Yudishṭhir
s.m. One who, in the name of religion, exercises care for a defenceless widow; a lawfully married man; a man who has a first and only wife
s.f. A lawful wife (one lawfully married and of the same caste as her husband)
adj. & s.m. Eminent in piety; — one who is eminent in piety
s.m. A seeker after dharm, q.v.; — adv. For the sake of establishing religion, &c.
s.m. Holy Scripture (as the Bible, the Qorʼān, &c.)
s.f. 'The scales of religion'; weighing or considering religion for the purpose of enquiry
s.f. Abandonment of religion, apostasy; abandonment of virtue, or duty, &c.
vulg. dharm-gyān, s.m. The knowledge of moral, legal, and religious duty
vulg. dharma-gya, adj. Knowing the law or what is right, knowing (one's) duty, conversant with virtue
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Observing the law, fulfilling (one's) duties, practising virtue, virtuous, dutiful, moral; — one who is observant of religion or duty, &c.; an honest spouse
s.f. A dutiful and virtuous wife
s.m., or dharm-ćintā, s.f. Pondering on the law or duty, study of virtue, consideration of moral duties, virtuous reflection
s.m. lit. 'Slave of duty,' &c.; an attendant upon a temple
s.m. Violation of law or right, injustice, oppression
adj. & s.m. Violating the law, or right, &c., wicked, immoral; — a wicked or unjust man, an infidel; a villain
s.m. An epithet of the chief light of a religion; e.g. Christ, Rāma, Moḥammad, &c.
s.m. A messenger in the cause of religion
adj. & s.m. Supporting or maintaining justice or the law, &c., virtuous, just, moral; — a maintainer of justice, &c.
adj. & s.m. Displaying the flag or colours of religion; — one who makes a livelihood by assumed devotion, a religious hypocrite or impostor
s.m. A mass or accumulation of dharm, q.v.

dharm-rāʼe, s.m. 'King of justice,' just king; an epithet of Yama or Pluto, and of Yudhishṭhir, and of Arjun; — a king (in general); (for dharm-rājya), a kingdom where justice is administered; ruling justly
s.f. Fostering religion, or law, &c.; zeal for religion (whether ceremonial or otherwise)
s.m. A guardian or patron of law, or justice, or religion
dharm-rūpī, adj. Having the form of virtue, &c., consisting of, or replete with, virtue; virtuous, righteous
adj. Obstructing the law, opposed to law or virtue, illegal, immoral; — a law-breaker, &c.
s.f. A court of justice, tribunal; an assembly of pious persons; a religious society
s.m. 'Pillar of righteousness'; an epithet of a very religious man
s.m. 'Place of justice'; name of a town
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to religion, &c.; — anything inherent in or relating to religion
s.f. A code or collection of laws (especially the work of some saint or divine person, as Manu, &c.)
adv. In good faith, honestly; solemnly
s.m. 'Barrier of justice'; an epithet of Śiva; name of a son of Aryak
s.m. A body or code of laws (esp. the code of Hindū law), jurisprudence
adj. Of or relating to the code of Hindū law; lawful

s.f. A court of justice; an edifice erected for pious purposes, a charitable institution, hospital, alms-house, monastery, a place of rest for travellers and pilgrims; — dharm-sīl, adj. Of a virtuous disposition, virtuous, just, pious
s.f. or dharm-śīlatva, s.m. Piety of disposition; virtuousness
adj. (f. -ī), Adopted by law; of or relating to law

s.m., or dharm-kārya, s.m., or dharm-kriyā (vulg. kiryā), s.f., or dharm-karm, s.m. A religious or virtuous act, work of merit, religious duty or exercise, any indispensable act of religion; righteous conduct, acting according to law
To perform a duty; to do justice; to practise virtue, do good; to give alms
s.m. The department or province of the law; — name of a plain in the north-west of India near Dehli (the scene of the great battle between the Kurus and Pāṇḍus, commonly called Kurukshetr)
v.n. To store up treasure in the future world by good works and a virtuous life, to work out (one's) salvation
s.m. The book of the law, the sacred scriptures (as the Bible, the Vedas, the Qorʼān, &c.; syn. dharm-pustak)
s.m. Observance of moral or religious precepts, accepting or following the law
s.m. = dharma-jnān, q.v.; — dharm-mūrtti, dharam-mūrat, s.m. 'Image of righteousness,' an epithet of respect (used by Brāhmans, &c. in addressing a Rājā or a Vaiśya)
s.m. The foundation of Hindū law and religion; the Vedas
adj. Consisting of law or virtue, made up or replete with virtue; moral, righteous
s.m. Attachment to virtue or religion, piety, virtue
adj. Attached to virtue; engaged in religious duties; pious, holy
s.f. Fulfilment or discharge of duty, moral or religious observance; duty
s.f. Firmness in religion; steadfastness in virtue
adj. Discussing the law or duty; contending or talking about religion
adj. Endowed with virtue, virtuous, pious, upright, just, lawful
vulg. dharm-upadeś (˚ma+up˚), s.m. Instruction in law or duty; a discourse on religion; moral or religious instruction
vulg. dharm-upadeśak, s.m. A teacher of the law, a gurū or spiritual preceptor
s.m. Contention or dispute about religion
s.m. A legal marriage (it is of five kinds, viz. Brāhma, Daiva, Arsha, Gandharb, and Prājapatya)
s.m. One who is acquainted with the law and religion
adj. Devoid of religion or virtue, unrighteous, faithless, worthless
s.m. A religious war, a crusade

def: s.m. A kind of snake ( = dhāman, q.v.); a species of tree. s.m. Dwelling, house, &c. ( = dhām, q.v.); — a troop, host; — state, dignity, majesty, glory; light, splendour, radiance, brilliancy.
= dharm-ātmā, q.v.s.v. dharm.
s.f. = S دهرمتو धर्मत्व dharmatva, s.m. Religion, virtue, righteousness, rectitude, probity, morality, piety; — inherent nature, peculiar property.
adj. Virtuous, just, holy, pious.
s.m. = S دهرمتا धर्मता dharmatā, s.f. Religion, virtue, righteousness, rectitude, probity, morality, piety; — inherent nature, peculiar property.
s. m. Incarnation of Dharma, god of justice; used as a term of respect.
adj. & s.m. Obeying the law; knowing (one's) duties; virtuous, just, upright, religious, pious, holy, godly; — a virtuous or just man, a pious or holy man.
adj. Righteous, just, virtuous, religious, pious, devout; faithful.
s.f. = S دهارمکتو धार्मिकत्व dhārmikatva, s.m. Righteousness, justice, virtuousness, &c.
s.m. = S دهارمکتا धार्मिकता dhārmikatā, s.f. Righteousness, justice, virtuousness, &c.
adj. & s.m. Completely harmonizing or agreeing with law or virtue; very virtuous or pious; — a very virtuous or pious person.
more: adj. Virtuous, inclined to the performance of duty.

def: adj. & s.m. Completely harmonizing or agreeing with law or virtue; very virtuous or pious; — a very virtuous or pious person.

ety: dhār˚ = Prk. धर(इ), or धरे(इ) = S. धारय(ति), caus. of rt. धृ
def: v.t. To hold, bear, carry; to have, keep; to uphold, support, sustain; — to owe; — to pour (water).
more: v. a. 1. ( धारण r. धृ Hold, bear) To hold, to bear, to have, to keep, to place, to owe, to sustain, to support, to uphold. 2. (from دهار or धारा ) To pour (water).

ety: dhār˚ = Prk. धार(इ), or धारे(इ), fr. S. धार
def: v.t. To pour (water, &c.).

ety: dhar˚ = Prk. धर(इ) = S. धर(ते) 'place,' and धर(ति), 'seize,ʼrt. धृ
def: v.t. To place, put, put down, deposit, lay, lay down; to pledge, stake; to put on, apply; to put (to), apply (the mind, or ear, &c.), to keep (at); — to have (in the hand), to hold, lay hold of, clutch, grasp, gripe, seize, to hold fast or pertinaciously; to take; to assume; to keep, retain; to catch, seize, apprehend, give in charge; to take (a side), espouse (a cause), entertain or hold (an opinion); to have, possess, hold, own, occupy, take possession of; to acquire, receive, obtain
more: v. a. 1. To put or set down; place; locate. 1. Dhare raho . Sit down. 2. Āp bhī yahīṅ dhare haiṅ! ( Fac .). 3. Yeh balā kaun apne sir dhare? 4. Mazdūrī to yahāṅ kareṅ, peṭ kahāṅ dhar āëṅ? My work's done here, my stomach must I carry there? (Said by a day laborer who should be paid on the spot.). 5. Tumhārī bāt dharī jāë, na uṭhāī jāë . Your statement can neither be accepted nor refuted. 2. ( dhar lenā ) To catch; hold; lay hold of; seize 3. To put in one's charge. Dhar jā, mar jā! Prov. Deposit and die! (The depositor's death is the banker's opportunity). 4. To pledge; stake. Sārī jāëdād ek hī dāoṅ par dhar dī. dharel' , n. f. A kept mistress; a concubine.

v. a. 1. To place, put down, lay, assume, put on, apply. 2. To give in charge. 3. To seize, catch, lay hold of, hold, keep. دهرنادينا يا بيٿهنا dharnā-denā or -baiṭhnā, A mode of extorting payment of a debt or compliance with any demand. See Asiat. Res. Vol. 4. Art. 22.
subs: v.t. (intens.), To put down, set or lay down; to pledge, stake, &c. ( = dharnā)
v.t. To put aside, put by, lay by; to keep, retain, take care of
v.t. (intens.) To lay hold of, take hold of, grasp, seize ( = dharnā)
v.t. To drag off or away by main force; to pull or haul away.

def: s.m. The act of sitting in restraint at the door of a debtor, by the creditor or his agent, to enforce the payment of a debt; sitting doggedly and fasting at the door of a temple, &c., to extort favours from the idol, &c., or to obtain compliance with some demand
subs: dhaṛnā denā, v.n. To enforce payment of a debt or compliance with a demand by sitting (or seating an agent) doggedly at the debtor's door; to extort favours from an idol, &c. by sitting doggedly and fasting at the door of a temple, &c.

def: v.t. = ḍhālnā, q.v. v.t. To pour out, shed, spill, tilt; to throw, cast; to cast (metal in fusion), to mould, fashion, form, shape, coin (money), frame or compose (a sentence, &c.); to aim or direct (speech, at), to speak (at, -par); (fig.) to do mischief (to), to injure, mar, spoil.

Compelling payment of a debt or compliance with any demand by sitting at one's door, or causing annoyance by obstructing the conveyance of water to the house, etc, etc.; dunning. 1. Dharnā de-ke baiṭhe haiṅ . 2. Hamre duārī par dharnā dele baisal bāṭe, e nā ki katahūṅ bahrāīṅ! Bhoj. The mendicant has taken up his station at my door as though he had nowhere else to go to. dharā jānā ,; E. dharānā , v. n. To be apprehended; imprisoned. Dāëm dāëm juā khelī ta ne dharāī? Bhoj. Gamble day after day, and not be caught? dharā ḍhakā, dharī ḍhakī , Something saved or laid by. Kuchh dharā ḍhakā ho to nikālo! dhar pakaṛ-ke , adv. Perforce; by force. dhar rakhnā , v. n. To keep; retain.

ety: dhāṛ˚ = Prk. धाड(इ) = S. ध्राड(ते), rt. ध्राड् (adopted fr. the Prk.; prob. a denom. of ध्रष्ट p.p.p. of ध्रज् 'to glide, flow)
def: v.t. To send forth, pour forth, pour out; to shed, &c. to cast (metal in fusion), to mould, &c. (see ḍhālnā, the more com. form); — to roar, to bellow, to thunder (in this sense usually dahāṛnā; cf. ḍakārnā).
v.n. To flow down, to come down; to come; to draw near, approach.

ety: dharan (i.q. dharnā)+ait = Prk. āʼitto = āyanto = āwento = S. āpayan, the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi
def: s.m. One who sits in restraint at a person's door to compel payment of a debt or compliance with a demand; a dun (see dharnā, and dharnā denā).
s.m. = next, q.v.
more: n. f. S. धरणी 1. See بهوم 1, 2. Dharnī ko bhār utāran kāran līnhe haiṅ autār , Bhajan. Kansā sahit Dusāsan Hari ne Rāvan ḍāre mār . 2. A beam. dharnī-dhar , n. m. lịt . earth supporter. 1. A mountain. 2. An epithet of Shesh-nāg , a serpent who upholds the earth, and of Vishnu in the form of a tortoise and a boar. dharnī-kand , n. m. An esculent root or bulb.
s. f. The earth. دهرني دهر धरणीधर dharṇī-dhar, s. m. 1. A mountain. 2. n. prop. Name of Shesh̤-nāg, a serpent, who upholds the earth: and of Vish̤ṇu in the form of a tortoise and of a boar.

ety: dharoh˚, prob. = dharāʼo, q.v.+ar or aṛ = Prk. अडी = S.अ+र+ई
def: s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
more: s. f. A trust, a charge, any thing given in charge, a deposite.
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

ety: dharoh˚, prob. = dharāʼo, q.v.+ar or aṛ = Prk. अडी = S.अ+र+ई
def: s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

def: s.f. = dharohar, q.v. s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

def: n. f. A deposit ( mānat , 2). Hamrā ihāṅ kā dharohar dhail bā ki je āī tekrā dehab? Mag. Do I keep a bank that I must hand over money to every body that asks for it. dharoṛ rakhnā , v. a. To place in one's charge, trust, or custody; to deposit.
s.f. contrac. of dharohaṛ, q.v.
s.f.(m.?) = dharohar, q.v. s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

ety: dhar(nā)+aut = āwat = Prk. आवती = S. आप्ति (i.e. आपि caus. augment+aff. ति)
def: s.f. = dharohaṛ, q.v. s.f. What is committed to the charge (of another, syn. amānat), charge, trust; deposit, pledge, pawn; — adv. As a trust, or deposit
more: s. f. (dakh.) 1. See عوض iﻌwaẓ. 2. See امانت amānat, Deposite, &c.
subs: (or dharoṛ) rakhnā, v.t. To commit to the charge (of), to place in trust, to deposit.

ety: past. conj. part. of dharnā and pakaṛnā
def: adv. By main force, by force.
s.m. Repressing, curbing, overcoming; overbearing conduct, violence.
s.m. = dhartā, q.v.
s.m. Discount, commission.
s.m. Holder, bearer, supporter, preserver; a debtor.
adj. To be held; to be upheld, be supported; to be had, be possessed; to be placed, be fixed.
s.f. The earth; earth, ground, land, soil, mould
more: n. f. S. धरित्री, Pālī. dharanī . See بهوم 1, 2. 1. Jītā raho! dhartī mātā bojh sahāre . Benediction. Mother earth support you and your days prolong! 2. Safaid dhartī, kālā bīj, bovan-hārā gāve gīt . Riddle. Ans. Kitāb a book. White earth strewn over with black seeds, The sower sows and sings or reads. 3. Āsmān ne ḍālā, dhartī ne jhelā . Prov. By the Heavens cast down, by earth sustained. dhartī bāhnā, jotnā, yā chīrnā , v. n. To plough; break up land. dhartī-patī , n. m. Landlord ( zamīṅdār ). dhartī kā phūl , n. m. Mushroom. dhartī yā dunyā ke parde par , adv. On the earth; in the world. dhartī mālā , n. Mother earth.
s. f. The earth. دهرتي کا پهول dhartī kā phūl, s. m. A mushroom.
subs: dhartī bāhnā or jotnā or ćīrnā
To plough the earth or land
On the earth's surface, on the earth
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa धरित्ती (dharittī) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit धरित्री (dharitrī).

def: s.m. A species of mainā (bird).
more: s. m. The name of a bird, a kind of mainā.

def: s.f. = dhaṛwā, q.v. s.m. A bird of the mainā kind ( = dhaṛuʼā).
s.m. A bird of the mainā kind ( = dhaṛuʼā).
more: s. m. The name of a bird, a kind of mainā.

def: n. m. A species of maina . (?).
s.m. A paṭwārī, or market officer, whose business is to weigh the grain, &c. brought into a market, or at the harvest (and whose perquisite is a dhaṛī, q.v.).
n. m. See tolā , 2.
v.t. To cause to be placed, &c.; to cause to be held or laid hold of, to have one seized, or apprehended, or given in charge, &c. (see dharnā).
more: v. a. Caus of dharnā q. v. To bring an action against; bring to trial; bring to the bar. Bākī ke dhebuā chaiṅ leb ta chhoṛab . Bhoj. I'll have the rest of my pice here before I let you go.
(caus. of دهرنا) v. a. To cause to place, &c.

def: (fr. dhasnā, q.v.), s.m.(f.?) A steep slope (as of a river's bank, or the side of a hill), any declivous place; glacis (of a fort); a battery, earthworks, fortification; a sterile sandy eminence; (local) red sterile soil.
more: n. 1. A sterile sandy eminence; a glacis. 2. Red sterile soil. 3. A fort; stronghold. 4. A battery.

def: s.f. Giving way, sinking; coughing, cough ( = dhāṅs).
more: n. f. A cough. dhasak'nā , v. n. 1. To cough. Mārc jhāṛū! dhasaktī phiregī . Wom. Beat her with a broom that she may cough all her life. 2. To fall or come down (a mud wall).
